title: globulea atropurpurea var. marginata
intro: Globulea atropurpurea var. marginata, also known as the Globularia maritima or Sea Globularia, is a plant species that belongs to the Globulariaceae family. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with a range of medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical applications, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
